content.text:
You are building the Meridian Academic Publishing & Fulfillment Portal — a full-stack, offline-first web application for an academic institution. The system serves four user roles (Student, Instructor, Academic Staff, Administrator) and handles the full lifecycle of academic content submissions, physical journal/publication order fulfillment, peer reviews, and after-sales case management, all from a single web interface that works without any internet connection.

The tech stack is fully specified:
- Frontend: Dioxus (Rust framework compiled to WebAssembly via Trunk). The UI is role-aware, meaning navigation items, buttons, and visible data all change based on the logged-in user's role.
- Backend: Rocket (Rust web framework) exposing REST-style endpoints. The frontend calls these endpoints over localhost or the local network.
- Database: MySQL, storing all persistent data including users, roles, permissions, orders, submissions, reviews, audit logs, and payment records.
- Both frontend and backend are Rust crates in a single Cargo workspace.

The architecture is intentionally decoupled — the Dioxus frontend and Rocket backend are separate crates that communicate over HTTP. This keeps them independently testable while still running on the same machine or local network.

MANDATORY folder structure — the automated evaluator checks these paths exactly, so do not rename or reorganize them:

repo/                        ← root of all code
repo/Cargo.toml              ← Cargo workspace manifest (lists backend, frontend, unit_tests, API_tests as members)
repo/Cargo.lock
repo/backend/                ← Rocket backend crate
repo/frontend/               ← Dioxus frontend crate
repo/unit_tests/             ← Rust unit test crate (run via: cargo test -p unit_tests)
repo/API_tests/              ← Rust integration/API test crate (run via: cargo test -p API_tests)
repo/docker-compose.yml      ← orchestrates MySQL + backend + frontend containers
repo/run_tests.sh            ← shell script that runs both test suites and exits non-zero on any failure

The docker-compose.yml must:
- Start MySQL 8 with a persistent named volume and a healthcheck
- Start the Rocket backend only after MySQL is healthy (depends_on with condition: service_healthy)
- Start the Dioxus frontend (either via Trunk dev server or serving pre-built WASM with nginx)
- Expose the backend on port 8000 and the frontend on port 8080
- Include a healthcheck for the backend (e.g. GET /health returns 200)

The run_tests.sh must:
- Check if .env exists; if not, copy .env.example to .env
- Run: cargo test -p unit_tests
- Run: cargo test -p API_tests
- Exit with a non-zero code if either suite fails

An .env.example file must exist in repo/ with all required environment variables (DATABASE_URL, ROCKET_PORT, ROCKET_ADDRESS, session timeout, token expiry, etc.).

Beyond these mandatory paths, you are free to structure the internal files of backend/, frontend/, unit_tests/, and API_tests/ however makes the most sense for the codebase. Use SQL migration files for the database schema.

{"file_path": "/home/eren/Documents/task4/Meridian_Academy/repo/backend/src/migrations/001_initial.sql", "content": "CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (
    id VARCHAR(36) PRIMARY KEY,
    email VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    password_hash VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    first_name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    last_name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    role ENUM('student', 'instructor', 'academic_staff', 'administrator') NOT NULL DEFAULT 'student',
    is_active BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT TRUE,
    created_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    INDEX idx_users_email (email),
    INDEX idx_users_role (role)
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS submissions (
    id VARCHAR(36) PRIMARY KEY,
    author_id VARCHAR(36) NOT NULL,
    title VARCHAR(500) NOT NULL,
    abstract_text TEXT NOT NULL,
    content LONGTEXT NOT NULL,
    submission_type ENUM('journal_article', 'conference_paper', 'thesis', 'book_chapter') NOT NULL,
    status ENUM('draft', 'submitted', 'in_review', 'revision_requested', 'accepted', 'rejected', 'published') NOT NULL DEFAULT 'draft',
    submitted_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    FOREIGN KEY (author_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    INDEX idx_submissions_author (author_id),
    INDEX idx_submissions_status (status)
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS orders (
    id VARCHAR(36) PRIMARY KEY,
    user_id VARCHAR(36) NOT NULL,
    publication_title VARCHAR(500) NOT NULL,
    quantity INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
    unit_price DECIMAL(10, 2) NOT NULL,
    total_price DECIMAL(10, 2) NOT NULL,
    shipping_address TEXT NOT NULL,
    status ENUM('pending', 'processing', 'shipped', 'delivered', 'cancelled') NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending',
    payment_status ENUM('unpaid', 'paid', 'refunded') NOT NULL DEFAULT 'unpaid',
    created_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    INDEX idx_orders_user (user_id),
    INDEX idx_orders_status (status)
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS reviews (
    id VARCHAR(36) PRIMARY KEY,
    submission_id VARCHAR(36) NOT NULL,
    reviewer_id VARCHAR(36) NOT NULL,
    rating INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    comments TEXT NOT NULL,
    recommendation ENUM('accept', 'minor_revision', 'major_revision', 'reject', '') NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
    status ENUM('assigned', 'pending', 'completed') NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending',
    created_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    FOREIGN KEY (submission_id) REFERENCES submissions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    FOREIGN KEY (reviewer_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    INDEX idx_reviews_submission (submission_id),
    INDEX idx_reviews_reviewer (reviewer_id)
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS cases (
    id VARCHAR(36) PRIMARY KEY,
    order_id VARCHAR(36) NOT NULL,
    reporter_id VARCHAR(36) NOT NULL,
    assigned_to VARCHAR(36) NULL,
    case_type ENUM('damaged', 'missing', 'wrong_item', 'refund', 'other') NOT NULL,
    subject VARCHAR(500) NOT NULL,
    description TEXT NOT NULL,
    status ENUM('open', 'in_progress', 'resolved', 'closed') NOT NULL DEFAULT 'open',
    priority ENUM('low', 'medium', 'high', 'urgent') NOT NULL DEFAULT 'medium',
    created_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    FOREIGN KEY (order_id) REFERENCES orders(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    FOREIGN KEY (reporter_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    FOREIGN KEY (assigned_to) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
    INDEX idx_cases_order (order_id),
    INDEX idx_cases_reporter (reporter_id),
    INDEX idx_cases_status (status)
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS case_comments (
    id VARCHAR(36) PRIMARY KEY,
    case_id VARCHAR(36) NOT NULL,
    author_id VARCHAR(36) NOT NULL,
    content TEXT NOT NULL,
    created_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    FOREIGN KEY (case_id) REFERENCES cases(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    FOREIGN KEY (author_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    INDEX idx_case_comments_case (case_id)
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS audit_log (
    id VARCHAR(36) PRIMARY KEY,
    user_id VARCHAR(36) NULL,
    action VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    details TEXT NULL,
    created_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    INDEX idx_audit_log_user (user_id),
    INDEX idx_audit_log_action (action),
    INDEX idx_audit_log_created (created_at)
)
"}

services:
  mysql:
    image: mysql:8
    container_name: meridian_mysql
    environment:
      MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD: ${MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD:-rootpassword}
      MYSQL_DATABASE: ${MYSQL_DATABASE:-meridian_academy}
      MYSQL_USER: ${MYSQL_USER:-meridian_user}
      MYSQL_PASSWORD: ${MYSQL_PASSWORD:-meridian_pass}
    ports:
      - "3306:3306"
    volumes:
      - mysql_data:/var/lib/mysql
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "mysqladmin", "ping", "-h", "localhost", "-u", "root", "-p${MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD:-rootpassword}"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 10
      start_period: 30s
    networks:
      - meridian_net

  backend:
    build:
      context: ./backend
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
    container_name: meridian_backend
    environment:
      DATABASE_URL: mysql://${MYSQL_USER:-meridian_user}:${MYSQL_PASSWORD:-meridian_pass}@mysql:3306/${MYSQL_DATABASE:-meridian_academy}
      ROCKET_PORT: 8000
      ROCKET_ADDRESS: 0.0.0.0
      ROCKET_SECRET_KEY: ${ROCKET_SECRET_KEY:-random_secret_key_change_in_production_at_least_32_bytes_long!!}
      JWT_SECRET: ${JWT_SECRET:-change_this_jwt_secret_in_production_must_be_long}
      TOKEN_EXPIRY_HOURS: ${TOKEN_EXPIRY_HOURS:-24}
      SESSION_TIMEOUT_MINUTES: ${SESSION_TIMEOUT_MINUTES:-60}
      RUST_LOG: ${RUST_LOG:-info}
    ports:
      - "8000:8000"
    depends_on:
      mysql:
        condition: service_healthy
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "curl", "-f", "http://localhost:8000/health"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5
      start_period: 30s
    networks:
      - meridian_net

  frontend:
    build:
      context: ./frontend
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
    container_name: meridian_frontend
    ports:
      - "8080:8080"
    depends_on:
      backend:
        condition: service_healthy
    environment:
      BACKEND_URL: http://backend:8000
    networks:
      - meridian_net

volumes:
  mysql_data:
    driver: local

networks:
  meridian_net:
    driver: bridge
"}

set -e

# Check if .env exists; if not, copy .env.example to .env
if [ ! -f .env ]; then
    if [ -f .env.example ]; then
        echo "No .env file found. Copying .env.example to .env..."
        cp .env.example .env
    else
        echo "ERROR: Neither .env nor .env.example found!"
        exit 1
    fi
fi

# Source the .env file
set -a
source .env
set +a

echo "========================================="
echo "Running Meridian Academy Test Suites"
echo "========================================="

FAILED=0

echo ""
echo "-----------------------------------------"
echo "Running unit tests..."
echo "-----------------------------------------"
if cargo test -p unit_tests; then
    echo "Unit tests PASSED"
else
    echo "Unit tests FAILED"
    FAILED=1
fi

echo ""
echo "-----------------------------------------"
echo "Running API tests..."
echo "-----------------------------------------"
if cargo test -p API_tests; then
    echo "API tests PASSED"
else
    echo "API tests FAILED"
    FAILED=1
fi

echo ""
echo "========================================="
if [ $FAILED -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "SOME TESTS FAILED"
    exit 1
else
    echo "ALL TESTS PASSED"
    exit 0
fi
"}

Here is the complete feature set. Every item must be implemented.

ROLES & ACCESS CONTROL
The system has four roles: Student, Instructor, Academic Staff, and Administrator. Access control is enforced at three levels — which menu items are visible, which buttons are active, and which data records are returned. This is full RBAC, not just UI hiding. Every permission change (granting or revoking a role, changing a permission) must be written to an immutable audit log recording who made the change, what changed, and when.

AUTHENTICATION & ACCOUNT MANAGEMENT
Login is local-only using username and password with salted hashing (no external auth providers). Sessions expire after 30 minutes of idle time. When a user forgets their password, an Administrator generates a one-time reset token that is physically printed or copied and handed to the user in person — the token expires after 60 minutes and can only be used once. When a user requests account deletion, the account enters a 30-day soft-delete hold before permanent removal, preventing accidental data loss. Users can request an "Export My Data" archive that downloads only their own records as a local file — they cannot access other users' data through this feature.

USER CENTER
Each user has a profile they can edit (name, contact info, username). They can manage US shipping addresses, but only one address can be marked as the default at a time. They can set a custom invoice title that appears on printable receipts. Notification preferences let users choose what they want to be notified about, but in offline mode only in-app inbox banners function — email and SMS options are shown in the UI but are disabled and clearly labeled as unavailable offline.

SUBMISSIONS
Students and instructors submit academic content using guided templates that combine structured form fields with file attachments. A submission is not a single upload — it goes through multiple rounds, and users can resubmit up to 10 times before the stated deadline. Every submission attempt is saved as a new version, and users can always browse the full version history with timestamps displayed in MM/DD/YYYY 12-hour format (e.g., 04/02/2026, 02:30:00 PM). When a user downloads a submitted file, the system applies a visible watermark to the document showing the requester's full name and the exact download timestamp. File uploads enforce a strict allowlist (PDF, DOCX, PNG, JPG only), a 25 MB per-file size limit, and basic safety checks using file signature verification (magic bytes) and SHA-256 checksum logging — no cloud-based virus scanning.

ORDERS & FULFILLMENT
The order system supports academic journal or publication subscriptions with three period options: monthly, quarterly, and annual. Each order can have multiple line items with individual quantities. When a single order contains publications from multiple series, staff can split it into separate orders per series, or merge multiple orders from the same subscriber into one. A reconciliation view lets staff compare what was expected to be delivered against what was actually received for each issue. Staff log fulfillment events against each issue: missing issues, reshipments, delays, discontinuations, and edition changes. Every logged event requires a reason field — it cannot be saved blank.

REVIEWS & AFTER-SALES
Users can post a review for a received publication. They can post an initial review and then one follow-up response within 14 days of the original — no more after that. Reviews support up to 6 image attachments, each capped at 5 MB. For problems with orders, users open after-sales cases with a type of return, refund, or exchange. Cases move through a defined status workflow: Submitted → In Review → Awaiting Evidence → Arbitrated → Approved/Denied → Closed. The system tracks SLA timers: staff must give a first response within 2 business days of case submission, and the target for full resolution is 7 business days. These timers are visible to both the user and staff.

CONTENT METADATA GOVERNANCE
When any content item is saved, the system automatically validates and processes its metadata. Title fields are capped at 120 characters and summary fields at 500 characters — saving fails with a clear error if these are exceeded. Tags and keywords are also validated for reasonable length limits. SEO fields (meta title, meta description, slug) are auto-generated from the content's existing text using deterministic rules — no AI or external services. A local sensitive-word dictionary (managed by Administrators) defines words or phrases that trigger either automatic replacement with a placeholder or an outright block. Blocked content does not publish — it enters a review queue where Academic Staff must explicitly approve it before it becomes visible.

PAYMENTS
The payment system is designed as a pluggable offline module. By default it records Cash, Check, and On-Account transactions. Posting is idempotent — submitting the same transaction twice does not double-charge. The system supports escrow-style holds (funds reserved but not finalized), refunds against prior transactions, and generates nightly reconciliation reports comparing expected vs. actual balances. Any third-party payment gateway integration is explicitly disabled unless an offline-compatible connector is installed and configured by an Administrator. The system automatically flags abnormal orders for manual review — specifically orders with unusually high quantities or accounts with repeated refund requests — and holds them until a staff member clears the flag.

TESTS
Write comprehensive tests covering: authentication flows and session expiry, RBAC enforcement at the API level, submission versioning and deadline enforcement, file validation (format, size, signature), order split/merge logic and reconciliation diff calculation, after-sales status transitions and SLA timer logic, content governance (length validation, sensitive-word detection, SEO generation), and payment idempotency and abnormal-order detection. Both unit_tests and API_tests crates should have meaningful coverage.

    pub file_data: String, // base64 encoded

    pub id: String,
    pub version_number: i32,
    pub file_name: String,
    pub file_size: i64,
    pub file_type: String,
    pub file_hash: String,
    pub submitted_at: Option<String>, // MM/DD/YYYY 12-hour format
}

    pub fn to_response(&self) -> SubmissionVersionResponse {
        let submitted_str = self.submitted_at.map(|dt| {
            dt.format("%m/%d/%Y, %I:%M:%S %p").to_string()
        });
        SubmissionVersionResponse {
            id: self.id.clone(),
            version_number: self.version_number,
            file_name: self.file_name.clone(),
            file_size: self.file_size,
            file_type: self.file_type.clone(),
            file_hash: self.file_hash.clone(),
            submitted_at: submitted_str,
        }
    }
}
"}
